summary: Closed, unmerged grcastle decomp PR that replaced three nonmatching placeholders with C implementations for grCastle_801CD960, grCastle_801CF750, and fn_801CFAFC, plus a recovered fn_801CFAFC prototype in grcastle.h. The PR claimed clean ninja and 100% fuzzy matches, and bot report showed three new matches totaling +288 bytes. Follow-up comments flagged unresolved pointer-math/type issues in grCastle_801CD960 and grCastle_801CF750 and said the work was consolidated into other PRs.

key_files.role: Implemented grCastle_801CD960, grCastle_801CF750, and fn_801CFAFC; introduced pointer arithmetic over Ground/GroundVars storage and a PAD_STACK(16) in grCastle_801CF750.
Added item forward include and replaced fn_801CFAFC UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declaration with a typed void prototype.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_801CFAFC prototype changed to void fn_801CFAFC(Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, f32)

what_changed.text: grCastle_801CD960 was decompiled from a placeholder into a do-while loop over 12 elements, calling grLib_801C9B6C on addresses computed from Ground user data at base offset 0xE0 with stride 0x14.

grCastle_801CF750 was decompiled from a placeholder into a function using arg1 to choose idx 0 or 1, PAD_STACK(16), a CollData x34_flags.b1234 check, and pointer arithmetic to adjust a float at offset 0xD0.
fn_801CFAFC was decompiled from a placeholder into a void function that writes 4 through gp->gv.castle2.xC4 as s16 and conditionally calls ftLib_80086A4C when ftLib_80086960(gobj) succeeds.
grcastle.h was updated to include the item forward header and expose fn_801CFAFC with recovered parameter types instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grcastle.h include and prototype changes

what_changed.text: The PR was closed with merged_at null, and an author comment states the work was consolidated into other PRs.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: issuecomment-4056023214 Consolidated into other PRs

smart_moves.text: The PR updated the owning header when fn_801CFAFC's signature was recovered, rather than leaving the old UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declaration.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_801CFAFC prototype changed in src/melee/gr/grcastle.h

smart_moves.text: grCastle_801CD960 used a compact do-while loop for the repeated 12-element operation instead of manually unrolling repeated calls.
The PR author explicitly called out the remaining pointer-math/type problem instead of presenting raw offset access as fully cleaned-up source.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: issuecomment-4034545536 Will fix pointer math soon
text_corpus: issuecomment-4034974200 GroundVars offsets and structural-change explanation

decomp_lessons.text: For stage GroundVars work, a function can match while still exposing unresolved type recovery: this PR's grCastle_801CD960 and grCastle_801CF750 relied on raw Ground pointer arithmetic because the current castle GroundVars fields did not describe the observed layout, according to the author's follow-up comment.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: raw pointer arithmetic in grCastle_801CD960 and grCastle_801CF750

decomp_lessons.text: When a decompiled body proves a public function signature, update the header prototype and necessary forward declarations in the same PR slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_801CFAFC body and grcastle.h typed prototype

decomp_lessons.text: A matching report can support text-section progress, but claimed 100% matches should still be weighed against reviewability concerns such as PAD_STACK and raw pointer arithmetic.
evidence_refs: pr_body: ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0

naming_conventions.text: The PR retained address-style names for grCastle_801CD960, grCastle_801CF750, and fn_801CFAFC rather than inventing semantic names.
Local variables used short role names where roles were clear, such as gobj, gp, cd, pos, idx, and item.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grCastle_801CD960, grCastle_801CF750, fn_801CFAFC signatures and locals

naming_conventions.text: No supported semantic rename for castle GroundVars fields was introduced; unresolved offsets remained expressed through existing fields or pointer math.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gp->gv.castle2.xC4 and raw casts at 0xE0/0xD0

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used a do-while loop with an s32 counter initialized to 0 and loop condition i < 12 for grCastle_801CD960.
Used raw byte-pointer arithmetic to reach Ground-relative offsets 0xE0, 0x14 stride, idx * 16 adjustment, and 0xD0 float access.
Used PAD_STACK(16) in grCastle_801CF750; the PR body and bot report claim the function matched, but no narrow objdiff excerpt beyond fuzzy match/report text is included.
Used a direct casted store through gp->gv.castle2.xC4 as s16 in fn_801CFAFC.

review_feedback.text: No formal review comments or reviews are present in the loaded slice.
Author follow-up acknowledged pointer math as an issue: 'Will fix pointer math soon.'
review_feedback.author: johnwinston

review_feedback.text: Author follow-up explained that replacing pointer math with struct field access was non-trivial because castle GroundVars lacked named fields for the observed offsets and would require shared Ground/GroundVars structural changes.
review_feedback.author: johnwinston

review_feedback.text: Author later stated: 'Consolidated into other PRs.'
review_feedback.author: johnwinston

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that consolidated PR #2248 grcastle work
Search fn_801CFAFC prototype Item_GObj Ground Vec3 HSD_GObj f32
Search castle GroundVars xC4 xD0 xE0 field recovery
Search PR comments mentioning 'Will fix pointer math soon'
Search grcastle GroundVars structural changes shared Ground/GroundVars

notes: Diff evidence is complete for the PR slice and supports changed-file and code-shape claims.
Verification evidence is limited to PR body claims and bot report; no objdiff logs, build logs, or reviewer validation are loaded.
There are no review comments or reviews in the loaded slice.
The PR is closed with merged_at null; the final comment says work was consolidated into other PRs, so this intake should not treat the exact diff as accepted source history.
Pointer-math rationale is from an issue comment attributed to the PR author but includes a '--Claude' signoff; it should be treated as PR evidence, not independent type truth.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototype updates should accompany recovered function signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2248 recovered fn_801CFAFC's body and changed grcastle.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to a typed void prototype with Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, and f32 parameters, adding the item forward include needed for the signature.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grcastle.h prototype and include changes

accepted_candidate_records.title: Matching code may still need type-structure follow-up
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2248 claimed 100% matches for grCastle_801CD960 and grCastle_801CF750, but follow-up comments identified remaining raw pointer arithmetic tied to missing or insufficient castle GroundVars structure fields.

source_update_candidates.title: grcastle GroundVars offsets may be under-typed
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2248 evidence suggests src/melee/gr/grcastle.c had unresolved Ground/GroundVars layout around castle data offsets 0xE0, 0xD0, and an idx * 16 adjusted Ground pointer in grCastle_801CD960 and grCastle_801CF750. The author stated that replacing pointer math would require structural changes to shared Ground/GroundVars types.
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate path fact for curator review only; PR was not merged and the comment is not sufficient to define the correct structure.

source_update_candidates.title: Pointer-math exceptions should be paired with type-recovery follow-up
source_update_candidates.text: When a code match requires raw pointer arithmetic because current structs do not describe observed storage, record the exact missing offsets/stride and route a type-recovery follow-up instead of treating the raw casts as final style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: raw pointer casts in two matched functions
source_update_candidates.reason: This is consistent with existing standards but may be useful as a more explicit source-corpus note if similar PRs recur.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the exact GroundVars layout interpretation from the PR comment as accepted fact; the PR was unmerged and no structural patch is included.
Do not treat PAD_STACK(16) in grCastle_801CF750 as accepted style; the slice contains match claims but no objdiff details explaining why the tactic was required.
Do not infer reviewer intent because there were zero review comments and zero reviews in the loaded evidence.
Do not mark this PR as merged or accepted; metadata shows merged_at null and the author comment says the work was consolidated into other PRs.
Do not promote the generated-with-Claude note into an authorship or quality conclusion beyond the fact that it appears in the PR body.
